parameters.jsCode:
const docs = [
    {
        "source":  "about.md",
        "text":  "# Crestview Realty — About & Services

Crestview Realty is a full-service residential real estate brokerage. We help people
**buy, sell, rent, and invest** in homes across the metro area, and we pair every client
with a dedicated agent who knows the neighborhood. We work by referral and repeat
business, so our whole model is built on responsiveness — most new inquiries get a
personal reply the same day.

## Who we help

### Buyers
First-time buyers, move-up buyers, and relocations. We handle the whole journey:
needs assessment, lender referral and pre-approval, home search and showings, offer
strategy, negotiation, inspection, and closing. Buyer representation is **free to the
buyer** — our commission is paid from the transaction at closing.

### Sellers
We price your home with a data-backed comparative market analysis (CMA), prep and stage
it, market it across the MLS and major portals, run showings and open houses, and
negotiate to close. Listing commission is competitive and discussed up front (see
financing & fees doc).

### Renters & landlords
We place qualified tenants and represent landlords on residential rentals, including
lease paperwork and move-in coordination.

### Investors
We help investors find buy-and-hold and value-add residential properties, run the basic
numbers (rent estimate, cap rate, cash-on-cash), and connect them with property managers.

## Areas we serve
We cover the metro core and surrounding suburbs — established family neighborhoods,
new-construction communities, downtown condos, and nearby commuter towns. If you tell us
the area and price range you're targeting, we'll let you know quickly whether it's a
market we actively work and pair you with the right agent. (See the **areas** doc for the
neighborhoods we cover most.)

## How working with us starts
1. You send a short inquiry (what you want to buy/sell/rent, your area, budget, timeline).
2. You get a fast, personal response and, if you're ready, a no-pressure consultation.
3. We map out next steps — for buyers that's usually pre-approval; for sellers it's a
   CMA and a walkthrough.

## What makes us different
- **Speed.** We respond fast because in real estate the first agent to reply usually wins
  the relationship — and buyers lose homes by waiting.
- **Honesty about fit.** If your timeline or budget isn't a match for the area you want,
  we'll tell you straight and help you adjust rather than waste your time.
- **One point of contact.** A dedicated agent, not a call-center handoff.
</content>
"
    },
    {
        "source":  "buying.md",
        "text":  "# Crestview Realty — The Home Buying Process

Buying a home with Crestview is designed to be clear and low-stress. Here's how it works
and what to expect at each step.

## Step 1 — Get pre-approved
Before serious shopping, talk to a lender and get a **mortgage pre-approval** (not just a
pre-qualification). Pre-approval tells you your real budget and makes your offers far
stronger — in a competitive market, sellers often won't consider an offer without one.
Cash buyers skip this but should be ready to show proof of funds. We can refer trusted
local lenders if you don't have one.

## Step 2 — Define your search
We sit down (in person or over a call) to nail down: target neighborhoods, must-haves vs.
nice-to-haves, property type (house, condo, townhouse), price range, and timeline. The
tighter this is, the faster we find the right home.

## Step 3 — Tour homes
We set up showings that fit your schedule and send you new listings as they hit the
market. In fast markets, being able to tour quickly is the difference between getting the
home and missing it.

## Step 4 — Make an offer
When you find the one, we build an offer strategy: price, contingencies (financing,
inspection, appraisal), earnest money, and timing. We advise based on comparable sales
and how competitive the listing is, then negotiate on your behalf.

## Step 5 — Under contract → inspection & appraisal
Once your offer is accepted, you're "under contract." You'll do a **home inspection**
(and can renegotiate or walk if there are serious issues), and your lender orders an
**appraisal** to confirm the home's value.

## Step 6 — Closing
Final loan approval, a walkthrough, signing, and you get the keys. Typical timeline from
accepted offer to closing is about **30–45 days** with financing, faster for cash.

## Costs to plan for (buyers)
- **Down payment:** as low as 3–5% on many conventional loans; 20% avoids private
  mortgage insurance (PMI). FHA and VA options exist for those who qualify.
- **Closing costs:** roughly **2–5% of the purchase price** (lender fees, title,
  escrow, taxes).
- **Earnest money:** a good-faith deposit (often 1–3%) that applies to your purchase.
- **Buyer's agent commission:** in most transactions this is paid through the deal at
  closing, not out of the buyer's pocket — we'll always confirm this for your specific
  deal up front.

## How fast can I buy?
If you're pre-approved and decisive, you can be under contract within days and closed in
about a month. The most common delays are waiting on pre-approval and indecision on the
search — getting those handled early is the single biggest time-saver.
</content>
"
    },
    {
        "source":  "selling.md",
        "text":  "# Crestview Realty — Selling Your Home

We sell homes for the best price the market will support, in the timeline you need, with
as little disruption to your life as possible.

## Step 1 — Comparative Market Analysis (CMA)
We analyze recent comparable sales, current competition, and market trends in your
neighborhood to recommend a **listing price**. Pricing right from day one matters more
than anything — overpriced homes sit, go stale, and ultimately sell for *less*.

## Step 2 — Prep & stage
We advise on repairs, decluttering, and light staging that actually move the needle on
price. We arrange professional photography (and video/3D tour for higher-value homes)
because most buyers see your home online first.

## Step 3 — List & market
Your home goes live on the **MLS** and syndicates to the major portals (Zillow,
Realtor.com, Redfin, etc.). We add it to our buyer network, run social and email
promotion, and host showings and open houses.

## Step 4 — Offers & negotiation
We review every offer with you — not just price, but contingencies, financing strength,
and timeline — and negotiate to maximize your net proceeds and certainty of closing.

## Step 5 — Under contract → closing
We manage inspection, appraisal, and the buyer's financing through to closing, keeping
the deal on track. Typical time from listing to closing is a few weeks of marketing plus
~30–45 days under contract, but it varies with the market.

## What it costs to sell
- **Listing commission:** competitive and agreed in writing before we list. Commissions
  are negotiable; we'll explain exactly what's included and how any buyer-agent
  compensation is handled for your sale.
- **Seller closing costs:** typically include title/escrow fees and prorated taxes.
- **Optional prep:** staging or repairs, only where they pay for themselves.

## What's my home worth?
The honest answer needs a CMA — automated online "estimates" are often off by a wide
margin because they can't see your home's condition, updates, or street. Send us your
address and a few details and we'll prepare a real valuation, no obligation.

## How fast can I sell?
Well-priced, well-presented homes in active neighborhoods often go under contract within
a couple of weeks. Pricing and presentation are the two levers that control speed —
that's where we focus.
</content>
"
    },
    {
        "source":  "financing.md",
        "text":  "# Crestview Realty — Financing & Fees

A plain-English guide to paying for a home and what working with us costs.

## Pre-qualification vs. pre-approval
- **Pre-qualification** is a quick estimate based on info you tell a lender. It's a
  rough guide, not a commitment.
- **Pre-approval** means the lender has verified your income, assets, and credit and will
  lend up to a stated amount. This is what you want — it sets your real budget and makes
  your offers credible. **Get pre-approved before you shop seriously.**

## How much do I need for a down payment?
- Many conventional loans allow **3–5% down**.
- **20% down** lets you avoid private mortgage insurance (PMI) and lowers your payment.
- **FHA** loans allow lower down payments for qualifying buyers; **VA** loans can offer
  0% down for eligible veterans/service members.
- The right answer depends on your finances and goals — a lender will lay out the options.

## Closing costs
Plan for roughly **2–5% of the purchase price** in closing costs (lender fees, title
insurance, escrow, appraisal, prepaid taxes and insurance). Some of these are
negotiable, and in some markets sellers contribute toward buyer closing costs.

## Cash buyers
Cash purchases skip the loan and appraisal steps, close faster (often 1–2 weeks), and are
very attractive to sellers. You'll need to show **proof of funds** with your offer.

## What does working with Crestview cost?
- **Buyers:** in most transactions, the buyer's agent commission is paid through the deal
  at closing, so representation is typically **free to the buyer**. We always confirm the
  exact arrangement for your specific purchase in writing before you make an offer.
- **Sellers:** listing commission is **negotiable** and agreed in writing before we list.
  We'll show you exactly what's included (photography, marketing, MLS, negotiation,
  transaction management) and how buyer-agent compensation is handled.

## Investors — the quick numbers we run
- **Cap rate** = net operating income ÷ purchase price.
- **Cash-on-cash** = annual pre-tax cash flow ÷ cash invested.
- **Rent estimate** vs. mortgage + taxes + insurance + maintenance to gauge cash flow.
We'll run a basic analysis on any property you're considering and connect you with a
property manager if you want it hands-off.

## The bottom line on budget
Your **pre-approval amount** and your **down payment** together set your real shopping
range. Sorting these out early is the single biggest thing you can do to move fast and
make winning offers.
</content>
"
    },
    {
        "source":  "areas.md",
        "text":  "# Crestview Realty — Areas We Serve

We actively work the metro core and the surrounding commuter belt. Below are the areas we
cover most, with the kind of buyer they tend to suit. If your target isn't listed, ask —
we likely still cover it or can refer a trusted partner.

## Downtown & Midtown
Condos, lofts, and townhomes for buyers who want walkability, nightlife, and a short
commute. Price points range from entry-level condos to premium high-rise units. Best for
first-time buyers, professionals, and downsizers.

## Westside & Garden District
Established, leafy family neighborhoods with single-family homes, good schools, and larger
lots. Popular with move-up buyers and families. Mid-to-upper price range.

## Northgate & the New-Construction Communities
Newer subdivisions with modern single-family homes and townhomes, amenities like pools and
parks, and builder incentives. Great for families wanting new construction and predictable
HOAs.

## Riverside & Old Town
Character homes, historic streets, and a mix of renovated and fixer-upper properties.
Suits buyers who want charm and are open to some renovation, plus value-add investors.

## Suburban Commuter Towns (Fairmont, Lake Hollow, Crestview Heights)
More space for the money — larger homes and lots a short drive from the core. Popular with
families prioritizing size, yards, and schools over a downtown commute.

## Investment focus areas
For buy-and-hold and value-add investors, Riverside, Old Town, and parts of Northgate
offer the best mix of rent demand and entry price. We'll run rent estimates and basic
return numbers on any specific property.

## Not sure which area fits?
Tell us your budget, must-haves, and how far you're willing to commute, and we'll point
you to the neighborhoods that actually match — and tell you honestly where your budget
will and won't stretch.
</content>
"
    },
    {
        "source":  "faq.md",
        "text":  "# Crestview Realty — FAQ

**What does Crestview Realty do?**
We're a full-service residential brokerage. We help people buy, sell, rent, and invest in
homes across the metro area, with a dedicated agent for every client.

**How do I get started?**
Send a short inquiry with what you're trying to do (buy, sell, rent, or invest), the area
you're interested in, your budget, and your timeline. You'll get a fast, personal
response — usually the same day — and we'll suggest next steps.

**Does it cost me anything to work with a buyer's agent?**
In most transactions, the buyer's agent commission is paid through the deal at closing, so
buyer representation is typically free to you. We confirm the exact arrangement in writing
before you make an offer.

**What's your commission to sell my home?**
Listing commission is negotiable and agreed in writing before we list. We'll explain
exactly what's included — pricing analysis, prep advice, professional photography,
MLS + portal marketing, showings, negotiation, and transaction management.

**How much do I need to buy a home?**
It depends on the loan. Many conventional loans allow 3–5% down; 20% avoids PMI. Plan for
2–5% of the price in closing costs too. The best first step is getting pre-approved so you
know your real budget. (See the financing doc.)

**Do I need to be pre-approved before looking?**
For serious shopping, yes. Pre-approval sets your real budget and makes your offers
competitive — in many markets sellers won't consider an offer without it. We can refer
trusted lenders. Cash buyers should be ready to show proof of funds.

**How long does it take to buy or sell?**
Buying: once you're pre-approved and decisive, you can be under contract in days and
closed in about 30–45 days (faster for cash). Selling: well-priced homes often go under
contract within a couple of weeks, then ~30–45 days to close.

**What areas do you cover?**
The metro core and surrounding suburbs — family neighborhoods, new-construction
communities, downtown condos, and nearby commuter towns. Tell us your target area and
price range and we'll confirm quickly whether it's a market we actively work.

**What's my home worth?**
We'll prepare a real comparative market analysis (CMA) based on your home's condition,
updates, and recent comparable sales — far more accurate than an automated online
estimate. Send your address and a few details, no obligation.

**Can you help investors?**
Yes. We help with buy-and-hold and value-add residential properties, run the basic
numbers (rent estimate, cap rate, cash-on-cash), and can connect you with property
management.

**How fast will someone get back to me?**
Fast — responsiveness is the core of how we work. Most inquiries get a personal reply the
same day, and urgent buyers/sellers are prioritized.

**Do you help with rentals?**
Yes — we place qualified tenants and represent landlords on residential rentals,
including lease paperwork and move-in coordination.
</content>
"
    }
];

const MAX = 600; // target chunk size in characters
const items = [];
for (const doc of docs) {
  const paras = doc.text.split(/
\s*
/).map(p => p.trim()).filter(Boolean);
  let buf = '';
  for (const p of paras) {
    const candidate = buf ? buf + '

' + p : p;
    if (candidate.length > MAX && buf) {
      items.push({ json: { content: buf, source: doc.source } });
      buf = p;
    } else {
      buf = candidate;
    }
  }
  if (buf) items.push({ json: { content: buf, source: doc.source } });
}
return items;

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: "models/gemini-embedding-001", content: { parts: [ { text: $json.content } ] }, outputDimensionality: 768 }) }}
